sections.label: Section 889
sections.original: πρός νυν θεῶν ἄκουσον , ὡς μαθοῦσά μου
sections.translation: Now , for the gods ’ love , listen , so that you may know

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: τὸ λοιπὸν ἢ φρονοῦσαν ἢ μωρὰν λέγῃς .
sections.translation: the rest from me before deciding whether I am sane or foolish .

sections.label: Section 891
sections.original: σὺ δʼ οὖν λέγʼ , εἴ σοι τῷ λόγῳ τις ἡδονή .
sections.translation: Speak on , then , if you find pleasure in speaking .

sections.label: Section 892
sections.original: καὶ δὴ λέγω σοι πᾶν ὅσον κατειδόμην .
sections.translation: Well , I will tell you all that I have seen . When I came to Father ’ s ancestral tomb , I saw that streams of milk had recently flowed from the top of the mound

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: πηγὰς γάλακτος καὶ περιστεφῆ κύκλῳ
sections.translation: and that his sepulchre was encircled with garlands of all flowers that grow . I was astonished at the sight , and peered about lest somehow someone should approach close to me . But when I perceived that all the place was in stillness ,

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: τύμβου προσεῖρπον ἆσσον · ἐσχάτης δʼ ὁρῶ
sections.translation:
I crept nearer to the tomb , and on the mound ’ s edge I saw a lock of hair , freshly severed .
And the very moment I see it — ah me !— a familiar image rushes into my mind , and I feel that I am looking at a token of him whom I most love , Orestes .

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: καὶ χερσὶ βαστάσασα δυσφημῶ μὲν οὔ ,
sections.translation: Then I lift it in my hands and make no sound of bad omen , but the tears of joy straightaway fill my eyes . Even now I know well , just as I knew then , that this fair tribute has come from no one but him . Whom else does that tomb concern , save me and you ?

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: κἀγὼ μὲν οὐκ ἔδρασα , τοῦτʼ ἐπίσταμαι ,
sections.translation: And I did not make those offerings , I know , nor did you . How could you , when you cannot leave this house even to worship the gods without later regretting it ? Nor , again , does our mother ’ s heart incline to do such deeds , nor could she have done so without our knowledge .

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔστʼ Ὀρέστου ταῦτα τἀπιτύμβια .
sections.translation: No , these offerings are from Orestes ! Come , dear sister , have courage ! Not always does the same fortune , it is true , attend the same individuals . Ours was once to be despised , but today , perhaps , will seal the promise of much good .

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: φεῦ , τῆς ἀνοίας ὥς σʼ ἐποικτίρω πάλαι .
sections.translation: Oh , what foolishness ! How I have been pitying you !

sections.label: Section 921
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ; οὐ πρὸς ἡδονὴν λέγω τάδε ;
sections.translation: What , is my news unpleasing to you ?

sections.label: Section 922
sections.original: οὐκ οἶσθʼ ὅποι γῆς οὐδʼ ὅποι γνώμης φέρει .
sections.translation: You do not know where on earth or into what dreams you wander .

sections.label: Section 923
sections.original: πῶς δʼ οὐκ ἐγὼ κάτοιδʼ ἅ γʼ εἶδον ἐμφανῶς ;
sections.translation: How could I not know what I have plainly seen ?

sections.label: Section 924
sections.original: τέθνηκεν , ὦ τάλαινα , τἀκείνου δέ σοι
sections.translation: He is dead , poor girl , and your

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: σωτήριʼ ἔρρει · μηδὲν εἰς κεῖνόν γʼ ὅρα .
sections.translation: salvation by him is gone . Do not look to him .

sections.label: Section 926
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινα · τοῦ τάδʼ ἤκουσας βροτῶν ;
sections.translation: Ah , miserable me ! From whom have you heard this ?

sections.label: Section 927
sections.original: τοῦ πλησίον παρόντος , ἡνίκʼ ὤλλυτο .
sections.translation: From the man who was present when he perished .

sections.label: Section 928
sections.original: καὶ ποῦ ʼστιν οὗτος ; θαῦμά τοί μʼ ὑπέρχεται .
sections.translation: And where is he ? Amazement steals over my mind .

sections.label: Section 929
sections.original: κατʼ οἶκον , ἡδὺς οὐδὲ μητρὶ δυσχερής .
sections.translation: He is inside , a welcome guest , not unpleasing to our mother .

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινα · τοῦ γὰρ ἀνθρώπων ποτʼ ἦν
sections.translation: Ah , misery ! Who , then , can have made those ample offerings to my father ’ s tomb ?

sections.label: Section 932
sections.original: οἶμαι μάλιστʼ ἔγωγε τοῦ τεθνηκότος
sections.translation: Most likely , I think , someone placed those gifts in memory of the dead Orestes .

sections.label: Section 934
sections.original: ὦ δυστυχής · ἐγὼ δὲ σὺν χαρᾷ λόγους
sections.translation: Oh , my misfortune ! And I was hurrying here

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: τοιούσδʼ ἔχουσʼ ἔσπευδον , οὐκ εἰδυῖʼ ἄρα
sections.translation: with such joyous nows , ignorant after all of our downfall . But now that I have arrived , I find fresh sorrows added to the old !

sections.label: Section 938
sections.original: οὕτως ἔχει σοι ταῦτʼ · ἐὰν δέ μοι πίθῃ ,
sections.translation: So it stands with you . Yet if you will be persuaded by me , you will lighten the load of our present trouble .

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: ἦ τοὺς θανόντας ἐξαναστήσω ποτέ ;
sections.translation: How can I ever raise the dead back to life ?

sections.label: Section 941
sections.original: οὐκ ἔσθʼ ὅ γʼ εἶπον · οὐ γὰρ ὧδʼ ἄφρων ἔφυν .
sections.translation: That is not what I meant ; I was not born so foolish .

sections.label: Section 942
sections.original: τί γὰρ κελεύεις ὧν ἐγὼ φερέγγυος ;
sections.translation: What do you urge , then , of those things that I am capable of doing ?

sections.label: Section 943
sections.original: τλῆναί σε δρῶσαν ἃν ἐγὼ παραινέσω .
sections.translation: That you be brave in executing what I recommend .

sections.label: Section 944
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἴ τις ὠφέλειά γʼ , οὐκ ἀπώσομαι .
sections.translation: If any good can be done , I will not refuse .

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: ὅρα , πόνου τοι χωρὶς οὐδὲν εὐτυχεῖ .
sections.translation: Remember , nothing succeeds without toil .

sections.label: Section 946
sections.original: ὁρῶ . ξυνοίσω πᾶν ὅσονπερ ἂν σθένω .
sections.translation: I know it , and I will share your burden with all my power .

sections.label: Section 947
sections.original: ἄκουε δή νυν ᾗ βεβούλευμαι ποεῖν .
sections.translation: Hear , then , in what way I have decided to take action . As for the support of friends , you yourself doubtless know that we have none . Hades has taken our friends away ,

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: ἀπεστέρηκε καὶ μόνα λελείμμεθον .
sections.translation: and we two are left alone . I , so long as I heard that my brother still lived and prospered , had hopes that he would yet come to avenge the murder of our father . But now that he is no more , I look next to you

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: ὅπως τὸν αὐτόχειρα πατρῴου φόνου
sections.translation:
and ask that you not flinch from aiding me , your sister , to slay our father ’ s murderer , Aegisthus . There — I can have no secrets from you anymore .
How long will you wait in indifference ? What hope is left standing , to which your eyes can turn ? Now you are right to complain

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: πλούτου πατρῴου κτῆσιν ἐστερημένῃ ,
sections.translation: that you are robbed of possession of your father ’ s estate ; now you may mourn that you have advanced this far in years without wedded love or bridal song . And do not cling to hopes that you will ever meet with such joys . The man , Aegisthus , is not so unthinking

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: Αἴγισθος ὥστε σόν ποτʼ ἢ κἀμὸν γένος
sections.translation: as ever to permit that offspring should shoot up from you or from me either to be a certain bane for himself . But if you will follow my plans , first you will win praise for piety from our dead father below , and from our brother , too ;

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: ἔπειτα δʼ , ὥσπερ ἐξέφυς , ἐλευθέρα
sections.translation:
next , you shall be called hereafter free , just as you were born , and shall find a worthy marriage . For noble natures draw the gaze of all .
Then do you not see what fair fame you will procure for yourself and for me , by obeying me ?

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: τίς γάρ ποτʼ ἀστῶν ἢ ξένων ἡμᾶς ἰδὼν
sections.translation: What citizen or stranger when he sees us will not greet us with praises such as these : Behold these two sisters , my friends ! They saved their father ’ s house , and at a time when their foes were firmly established , they took their lives in their hands and administered bloodshed ! Worthy of love is this pair , worthy of reverence from all . At festivals , and wherever the citizenry is assembled , let these two be honored by all men for their manly courage . Thus will every one speak of us ,

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: ζώσαιν θανούσαιν θʼ ὥστε μὴ ʼκλιπεῖν κλέος .
sections.translation:
so that in life and in death our glory shall not fail .
Come , dear sister , be persuaded ! Toil with our father , share the burden of your brother , put an end to my troubles and an end to yours , keeping in mind that a shameful life brings shame upon the noble-born .

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: ἐν τοῖς τοιούτοις ἐστὶν ἡ προμηθία
sections.translation: In a crisis such as this , forethought is an ally both to those who speak and those who listen .

sections.label: Section 992
sections.original: καὶ πρίν γε φωνεῖν , ὦ γυναῖκες , εἰ φρενῶν
sections.translation: Yes , I agree . And before she spoke , my friends , if she were blessed with a sound mind , she would have remembered caution , even as she does not now .

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: ποῖ γάρ ποτʼ ἐμβλέψασα τοιοῦτον θράσος
sections.translation: Where can you have turned your eyes , that you arm yourself with such rashness and call me to serve beneath you ? Do you not see ? Your nature is a woman ’ s , not a man ’ s , and the strength of your hand is less than that of your adversaries . And their fortune prospers day by day ,

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: ἡμῖν δʼ ἀπορρεῖ κἀπὶ μηδὲν ἔρχεται .
sections.translation: while ours ebbs and comes to nothing . Who , then , plotting to subdue such a man , would escape destruction unharmed ? See to it that , badly as we fare now , we do not acquire greater evil , if any one hears this talk of yours .

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: λύει γὰρ ἡμῖν οὐδὲν οὐδʼ ἐπωφελεῖ
sections.translation: It brings us no relief or benefit , if after winning fair fame we die an ignominious death . For death is not the most odious thing ; it is rather craving death , but lacking the means to die . No , I plead with you , before we are utterly , totally destroyed

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: ἡμᾶς τʼ ὀλέσθαι κἀξερημῶσαι γένος ,
sections.translation: and before we leave our house desolate , restrain your rage ! I will take care that your words remain secret and harmless for you . You in turn must get hold of good sense at long last and yield to the powerful since you have no strength .

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: πείθου · προνοίας οὐδὲν ἀνθρώποις ἔφυ
sections.translation: Listen to her . There is no better gain for mortals to win than foresight and a prudent mind .

sections.label: Section 1017
sections.original: ἀπροσδόκητον οὐδὲν εἴρηκας · καλῶς δʼ
sections.translation: You have said nothing unexpected . Well I knew that you would reject what I proposed . But the deed must be done with my own hand , by me and me alone .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: τοὔργον τόδʼ · οὐ γὰρ δὴ κενόν γʼ ἀφήσομεν .
sections.translation: I certainly will not leave it unaccomplished .

sections.label: Section 1021
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! If only you had had such resolve on the day of our father ’ s death ! Then you would have accomplished everything !

sections.label: Section 1023
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἦ φύσιν γε , τὸν δὲ νοῦν ἥσσων τότε .
sections.translation: My nature was the same then , but my mind was less ripe .

sections.label: Section 1024
sections.original: ἄσκει τοιαύτη νοῦν διʼ αἰῶνος μένειν .
sections.translation: Strive to keep your mind that way through all your life .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: ὡς οὐχὶ συνδράσουσα νουθετεῖς τάδε .
sections.translation: You make these admonitions like one who will not assist me .

sections.label: Section 1026
sections.original: εἰκὸς γὰρ ἐγχειροῦντα καὶ πράσσειν κακῶς .
sections.translation: I will not , for he who sets hand to the deed is likely also to suffer disaster .

sections.label: Section 1027
sections.original: ζηλῶ σε τοῦ νοῦ , τῆς δὲ δειλίας στυγῶ .
sections.translation: I admire you for your prudence . For your cowardice I hate you .

sections.label: Section 1028
sections.original: ἀνέξομαι κλύουσα χὤταν εὖ λέγῃς .
sections.translation: I will listen no less calmly when you praise me .

sections.label: Section 1029
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔ ποτʼ ἐξ ἐμοῦ γε μὴ πάθῃς τόδε .
sections.translation: Never fear to suffer that from me , at least .

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: μακρὸς τὸ κρῖναι ταῦτα χὠ λοιπὸς χρόνος .
sections.translation: There is time enough in the future to decide that .

sections.label: Section 1031
sections.original: ἄπελθε · σοὶ γὰρ ὠφέλησις οὐκ ἔνι .
sections.translation: Leave ! You have no power to help .

sections.label: Section 1032
sections.original: ἔνεστιν · ἀλλὰ σοὶ μάθησις οὐ πάρα .
sections.translation: No , I have it , but you lack the ability to listen and learn .

sections.label: Section 1033
sections.original: ἐλθοῦσα μητρὶ ταῦτα πάντʼ ἔξειπε σῇ .
sections.translation: Go , reveal everything to your mother !
